purpose.value: To describe the requirements specifications for applications and a data repository designed to distribute manufacturing data, enabling researchers, practitioners, and solution providers to interact with real data generated from a manufacturing facility.
purpose.source_quote: The purpose of this SRS document is to describe the requirements specifications for applications and a data repository to distribute manufacturing data.

stakeholders.value: Interested end-users (Researchers, Practicing Engineers, Software Developers)

context.value: The system is part of a larger manufacturing-monitoring system. It addresses the need to automate the discovery, curation, and integration of manufacturing data, which originates from a network of industrial equipment instrumented with sensors and data-acquisition devices. The data distribution must support real-time monitoring (VDS) and object-of-interest observation (QDR).
context.source_quote: Distributing manufacturing data across an enterprise requires the curation and management of the data within a repository and end-user services to access the data.

in: Volatile Data Stream (VDS) application for streaming time-synchronized manufacturing data.
Query-able Data Repository (QDR) application for querying and sharing structured manufacturing data.
Web service interface for data distribution.
Administrative functions for managing users, groups, and data templates.
out: Manual discovery, curation, and integration of manufacturing data by users.

capabilities.value: Stream time-synchronized manufacturing data in compliance with MTConnect standard (VDS).
Allow users to query stored manufacturing data via a template-driven web-based form, endpoint query, and/or RESTful API call (QDR).
Provide administrative functions to CRUD manage users, groups, permissions, and data templates.
Curate and store manufacturing data into a database according to predefined data-structure templates.
Serve all data and administrative functions over a RESTful API.
Provide a User Interface (UI) for administration and querying.

external_interfaces.value: Industrial Equipment Network (Source of manufacturing data)
Database (for QDR storage and template management)
Web Browsers (Client-side UI, supporting Safari 7+, Chrome 44+, IE 10+, Firefox 40+)

constraints.value: Server-side components must operate on a Linux operating system environment.
Client-side communication must use HTTPS/SSL/TLS with a minimum encryption level of 128 bits.
QDR must be able to curate data collected in batches from the Industrial-Equipment Network.
QDR must process curated data daily to populate a list of available elements for query.

success_criteria.value: VDS must provide available fresh data at a rate of no less than 1 Hz.
QDR response time must be minimized using industry recommended practices.
System must pass automated dynamic application security testing.

assumptions: The 'industry recommended practices' for performance, reliability, and security are sufficiently defined by the customer/NIST context, even if not explicitly detailed in the SRS.
The existence of a functional 'Industrial-Equipment Network' capable of generating data conforming to MTConnect standards.
The customer is responsible for the setup, hosting, host-security configuration, and administration of the QDR application.
clarifying_questions: What specific industry standards or metrics define 'industry recommended practices' for QDR response time and general performance?
What is the required data retention period for the QDR?
What are the specific requirements for the 'MTConnect Agent' implementation beyond the standard documentation reference?
Are there specific compliance requirements (e.g., ISO standards) beyond general security practices that must be met for the manufacturing data?

candidate_archetypes.why: The core functionality relies on exposing capabilities (VDS, QDR) via versioned, RESTful APIs.
It involves ingesting (VDS/QDR Capture), transforming (data curation), and delivering data into a repository.
It is a multi-tenant, browser-delivered transactional application with server-side business logic and a persistent datastore.
The QDR relies heavily on a database engine for persistence and indexing.

salient_domains.why: The entire system revolves around the ingestion, storage, and retrieval of manufacturing data.
The system must fulfill specific functions: streaming, querying, and administration.
Specific requirements exist for VDS streaming rate (1 Hz) and minimized QDR response time.
Mandates HTTPS, least privilege, and passing automated security testing.
Requires 95% uptime, though formal MTBF/MTTF are not specified.
Defines constraints on fixing security defects (max two person days).
Must adhere to MTConnect standards and support specific browser versions.
Implied by security requirements and the handling of potentially sensitive manufacturing data.
